database_info.name: Credit Hire Industry Directory
database_info.description: Directory of companies, practitioners and services active in the credit hire sector

entries.name: 4th Dimension Innovation
entries.description: 4th Dimension Innovation Ltd (“4th-D”) was founded in 2003 and has achieved spectacular growth every year since. Initially formed to tackle the costs and delays associated with motorcycle repair, the scale of operations and the range of services offered have expanded significantly. Today they offer a full claims handling service from first notification through to subrogated loss recovery

entries.name: Aardvaark
entries.description: Aardvark claim over 40 years’ collective experience in the motor insurance industry and specifically in the hire and mobility sectors working predominantly in motor insurance claims. They claim to know what makes their customers happy and also what you really don’t like about the insurance industry.  Both of the directors were former employees at Accident Exchange.

entries.name: ACH
entries.description: ACH supply credit-hire services for drivers of mainstream and prestige cars, commercial vehicles and motor bikes. They have been in business since 1995 and are subscribers to the ABI GTA. They are also authorised and regulated by the FCA and members of the CHO.

entries.name: Active Claim Solutions
entries.description: Active Claims Solutions was formed in 2007 and is part of the award-winning Ogilvie Group which includes Ogilvie Fleet. Over thirty years that business has become a contract hire, leasing and fleet management solution provider for UK and European companies operating fleets of all sizes.  Active Claim Solutions specialises in the provision of outsourced motor claims management and they report handling claims for insurers, brokers and fleets across the UK by supplying tailored services to meet the needs of their customers. They are a subscriber to the GTA and a member of the CHO and are authorised and regulated by the FCA.

entries.name: Alpha Accident Management
entries.description: Alpha Accident Management is a division of AAM (NI) Ltd. They are a family owned and family run business based in Dungannon, County Tyrone. They supply credit-hire across Northern Ireland using a mix of their own and outsourced fleet which includes licensed taxi vehicles, prestige, 4x4 and mainstream cars and commercial vehicles. The business was started in 2007 and the company website claims that they have helped over 2,000 individuals since then. Alpha Accident Management is a member of the CHO.

entries.name: Anexo Group PLC
entries.description: In contrast with Redde, and indeed with most other large CHOs, Anexo is an integrated credit-hire and legal services group which acts predominantly on behalf of impecunious claimants. They supply replacement vehicles at commercial credit-hire rates and consider litigation to be at the heart of their operational strategy. The Group uses its in-house litigators to recover hire and repair charges from the at-fault motorist’s insurer.

entries.name: Auto Claims Assist
entries.description: Auto Claims Assist is an accident management company based in Manchester. Established in 2009, they report working with an established panel of solicitors and to have a comprehensive fleet of vehicles from standard to prestige and vehicles for private hire. Auto Claims Assist are a member of the CHO.

entries.name: Auto Logistic Solutions
entries.description: Auto Logistic Solutions (ALS) was formed in 2010 by Kyle Harris when he parted company with Britannia Accident Assist. With 3 key staff members ALS began to grow quickly, doubling in size within the first year. The management team now have over 120 years combined experience. Their expertise covers all aspects of claims management, from front end claims processing, vehicle distribution and provision, through to un-insured loss recovery, litigation and financial compensation. ALS pride themselves on providing an un-paralleled level of service to all motor claims sectors, be it through vehicle repair outlets, motor dealerships, insurance brokers or fleet operators. It is this dedication to going the extra mile that has enabled ALS to provide what they believe to be an innovative products not seen anywhere else in the industry, resulting in steady double-digit growth year on year.

entries.name: Auxillis
entries.description: Redde Northgate, through its various subsidiaries, provides a range of accident management, incident management and legal services including vehicle replacement and repair management together with full claims-handling assistance, as well as legal and other bespoke services. The business was founded in 1992 as Helphire plc and now works predominantly with insurance companies, insurance brokers and other motoring organisations such as car dealerships, motor manufacturers, leasing companies, repair centres and large national fleet owners.

entries.name: AX
entries.description: AX, formerly known as Accident Exchange, now market their products under the brand names AX Automotive and AX Innovation. They cite their target sectors as dealerships and body shops, motor insurers, brokers and accident management companies. Their services are categorised under the labels AX Retain, AX Replace, AX Repair, AX Manage, AX Connect and AX Protect. Full details can be found on their website.

entries.name: Bristol & London
entries.description: Established in 1998 and a member of the CHO, Bristol & London was originally a specialist credit-hire provider for clients driving prestige and exotic vehicle. More recently, it has recognised that there is a market for people looking to rent a prestigious car for holidays or business trips and not simply to replace a car that has been involved in an accident. They have, therefore, made their fleet available on the conventional daily rental market.

entries.name: Central Cab Care
entries.description: Established in 2003, Central Cab Care is one of the leading taxi credit-hire companies in the UK. They have a fleet of vehicles, including black cabs, saloons, estates, MPVs, mini-buses, prestige and disabled-access vehicles. All vehicles are supplied to the customer specified and licensed for the relevant taxi licensing local authority and come with full insurance cover and roadside assistance. Central Cab Care supply licensed vehicles nationwide, matching vehicles to the requirements of over 370 local authorities.  They are members of the CHO.

entries.name: Chief Vehicle Rentals
entries.description: Chief Vehicle Rentals was founded in 1994 with a small loan from The Prince’s Trust. Initially a car rental business working in Torbay, the business has grown significantly and now includes three interconnected strategic business units - a vehicle rentals division, a corporate fleet division and an accident replacement vehicles division.  The company is a member of the British Vehicle Rental & Leasing Association, a United Rentals System licensee, a member of the CHO and subscribes to the GTA. They are also authorised and regulated by the FCA.

entries.name: Collision Solutions
entries.description: Collision Solutions have over 20 years’ experience within the accident management industry. Based in the North West of England they offer nationwide coverage for both private owners and corporate fleet. They can supply a complete range of vehicles from Minis to Range Rovers as well as a full range of commercial vehicles. They are a member of the CHO and a subscriber to the GTA.

entries.name: Concierge Claims Management
entries.description: The team at CCM have a wealth of experience in the management of claims covering a wide variety of vehicle manufacturers. This includes; prestige, sports, electric and chauffeuring vehicles and CCM understand the importance of a manufacturer approved repairer, as well as providing the client with a like for like replacement vehicle whilst their vehicle is off the road.

entries.name: Crash Services
entries.description: Crash Services supply vehicle recovery, collision investigation, repair management, credit-hire services and legal and medical help for claims. They work from locations in Belfast, Newry and Derry/Londonderry and hold ISO 9001 Quality Accreditation. They also supply an annual UK & Ireland Breakdown service to over twenty thousand members.

entries.name: Crystal Claims Management
entries.description: With over 10 years’ experience in the accident management industry, Crystal Claims Management offers a comprehensive accident management and credit-hire service. They specialise in supplying like-for-like PCO/Taxi plated, mainstream, prestige, executive and commercial vehicles.

entries.name: Edam Group
entries.description: Founded in 2001, Edam Group Limited (“Edam”) owns the entire issued share capital of several businesses including Easi-Drive Limited, Octopus Marketing Limited, Credit Repair Limited, Blue Box Insurance PCC Limited and Garvins Law limited.  Of those companies, Easi-Drive is the provider of credit-hire services, Credit Repair Limited the funder of credit-repair services and Garvins Law was the legal entity engaged in collecting debts which did not settle within the terms of the GTA protocol.

entries.name: Enterprise
entries.description: Enterprise is the largest privately-owned car hire company on the planet. In FY 2019 it recorded global revenue of $25.9b. It employs 100,000 people and manages almost 1.9 million vehicles spread across more than 450 vehicle makes and models. It operates out of more than 10,000 fully staffed neighbourhood and airport locations in more than 90 countries and territories. In the USA, Enterprise dominates the provision of replacement or loan vehicles to customers whose car is being serviced or repaired as well as being a full-service rental provider.  They opened their first European rental office on Sept. 23, 1994 and now offer a range of services including retail and corporate rental, as well as credit-hire, credit-repair and intervention services whereby they will offer insurers access to their national fleet at rates which respect the level of business generated.

entries.name: Independent Claims Recovery
entries.description: Independent Claims Recovery Ltd are members of The CHO and appear to be a firm of solicitors based in Bromsgrove, Worcestershire

entries.name: Integrity Claims Management
entries.description: Founded by Russell and Steve Ryan, Integrity Claims Management claim experience of credit-hire over a quarter of a century. They report owning an extensive fleet of replacement vehicles, a recovery fleet and repairing garages, as well as having their own legal team. They also report marketing a series of insurance policies under the brand ‘Integrity Club 20’.  Integrity Claims Management is a member of the CHO and subscribes to the ABI GTA.

entries.name: Lighthouse Claims
entries.description: Lighthouse Claims own and maintain a fleet of high-quality prestige vehicles and are solely dedicated to providing their clients with a seamless and professional service. They can accommodate delivery throughout the UK.

entries.name: Magna Accident Services
entries.description: Magna Accident Services is a trading style of Magna Accident Services Limited, a family owned company based in Lincolnshire. They offer post-accident services including first notification of loss, repair management and credit-hire. The directors have been in the credit-hire industry for many years, previously having specialised in the provision of replacement taxis.   The Company provides full uninsured loss recovery services, including loss of revenue claims if applicable and works with a nationwide network of body shops which can repair all types of vehicles, including large commercial vehicles.

entries.name: Motor Claims Network
entries.description: Motor Claims Network report 25 years combined experience in the motor insurance industry and a reputation for outstanding customer service. They offer credit-hire and credit-repair services nationwide and are a member of the CHO.

entries.name: MSL
entries.description: MSL is a trading name of MSL Legal Expenses Limited and MSL Vehicle Solutions Limited and offers claims handling services, legal expenses insurance and assistance to MGAs, insurers, brokers & self-insured businesses. It is part of Drive Further which also includes Dualdrive, Financial & Legal Insurance and Supportis.   Drive Further is a family owned collective, now owned by Nick Garner. Through its various businesses, it also supplies a range of services to brokers, intermediaries, SMEs, insurers, solicitors and their customers.

entries.name: Novo
entries.description: Since 2015 NOVO have been supporting the transition to EV Mobility through a range of innovative insurance, claim and mobility solutions...

entries.name: Pearl Assistance
entries.description: Pearl Assistance have been helping accident victims since 2009.  Strategically based in Stratford, London, they supply replacement vehicles throughout London, and across the entire UK.  They report that they can supply any type of replacement car, but occupy a niche in helping minicab, chauffeur and taxi drivers get back on the road after an accident.

entries.name: Proximo
entries.description: Proximo was formed in 2003 and claim to offer bespoke incident management solutions to the insurance industry with a single fulfilment platform controlling all aspects of the client journey including vehicle provision, ULR facilities, and legal support. Proximo market a range of motor legal expense insurance products, under the Greenlight brand and manage a repair network with 140 approved car and light commercial repairers that are strategically placed across the UK. Proximo are authorised and regulated by the FCA.

entries.name: RTA Assistance
entries.description: RTA Assistance work with insurance brokers, body shops, fleet operators and the taxi industry to supply credit-hire and repair services. They have been involved in the accident management industry for thirty years, are members of the CHO, subscribers to the ABI GTA and are authorised and regulated by the FCA.

entries.name: S&G Response
entries.description: S&G Group reports that the Company has two operating divisions. The Credit Services department supplies traditional “non-fault” claims handling services including credit-hire and credit-repair and those activities accounted for 10% of the total claim volume processed during 2018. The Insurance Services department provides 1st party indemnity spend services and 3rd party cost containment solutions. This division accounted for 90% of claim volumes handled.

entries.name: Simple Self Drive
entries.description: Simple Self-Drive Ltd is a provider of credit-hire to motorists throughout London claiming 15 years’ experience in the motor insurance industry. In simple terms, the Company hires like-for-like replacement vehicles ready for use while a client’s damaged vehicle is off the road or at the garage getting repaired. Simple Self-Drive are members of the CHO.

entries.name: Spectra Drive
entries.description: From its Manchester based operations Spectra Drive offer a 24/7 nationwide range of mobility, repair and legal service solutions through a national repair network and using a panel of legal experts. They were incorporated after they bought the assets of Spectra Vehicle Rental Limited in June 2017. They subscribe to the ABI GTA and are members of the CHO.

entries.name: Speedy Claims
entries.description: Established in 2013, the skilled team has over 20 years’ experience in the complexities of accident management and can provide expert advice to both their clients and extended network of partners.  Speedy Claims is a specialist credit hire provider of Private Hire and Hackney Carriage Taxi vehicles to non-fault accident victims. They are based in Milton Keynes.

entries.name: Strada Assist
entries.description: Strada Assist offer an end to end solution for private hire and taxi drivers following the loss of use of their vehicle throughout the UK. They claim that they are recognised as a leading provider of replacement vehicles and accident management services for the taxi and private hire sector. They are not subscribers to the GTA. They have access to a range of vehicles which include taxi, PCO, MPV’s, manual, automatic, specially adapted vehicles for the disabled and dual control vehicles. Their service also includes vehicle supply for high risk drivers and those under 21.

entries.name: The CAMS Group
entries.description: The CAMS Group is an outsource provider of fleet management services. They report a fleet of more than 250 vehicles with which they deliver a nationwide service via strategically placed depots. As well as supplying a credit-hire service, they also offer intervention services and, through TFS Vehicle Leasing offer personal and business contract hire services. The Group also offers several insurance products including Guaranteed Vehicle Hire, GAP Insurance, Excess Protection, Breakdown Cover, Legal Expense Insurance and Smart Repair Protection against scratches and dents. The CAMS Group is a member of the CHO.

entries.name: Veezu Assist
entries.description: Veezu Assist is a specialist claim management service helping private hire drivers with accident claims. The company is a member of the CHO.   Veezu’s parent company, Veezu Holdings, is the UK’s leading multi-region private hire company connecting thousands of driver partners to millions of passengers around their regional UK hubs.

entries.name: Vision Vehicle Solutions
entries.description: Vision is an established provider of credit-hire and intervention hire, to motorists involved in road traffic accidents. They have over 20 years’ experience and are part of Markerstudy Group. They are authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) and a member of the CHO.  Vision report running a varied vehicle fleet, ranging from standard small cars, through to sports, prestige, 4x4 and commercial vehicles. Taxis, motorcycles and other specialist vehicles are also available through relationships with partner suppliers. National coverage is offered with vehicle deliveries and collections available 7 days a week. In addition, and perhaps unsurprisingly given their ultimate parent, Vision report having protocol agreements with most UK insurers.

entries.name: Bentley Sterling
entries.description: Amongst other things, Bentley Sterling & Co specialise in all aspects of Credit Hire Litigation and claim a vast experience within their team.   They offer legal services, whether at an organisational level or for individuals/claimants. They work both diligently and robustly to ensure reasonable recovery of credit hire charges in both fast-track and multi-track claims. They are at the forefront of ensuring they are in tandem with the changes in law in this area.  See more..

entries.name: Bike Assist Legal
entries.description: Bike Assist Legal specialises in representing motorcyclists and scooter riders who have been involved in road traffic accidents and who have been injured and/or suffered damage to their motorcycles/scooters as a result of a road traffic accident.

entries.name: Bond Turner
entries.description: Bond Turner Solicitors is a niche, multi-disciplined law firm specialising in personal injury, credit-hire, clinical negligence, defamation, professional negligence, and historic abuse claims. Based in the northwest of England with offices in Liverpool and Bolton, they support clients nationwide with over 100 Solicitors, legal executives and litigators as well as in-house barristers, interpreters, and accountants. They also provide access to expert medical and rehabilitation care and can help with vehicle recovery and replacement following an accident. Bond Turner are owned by Anexo-Group plc.

entries.name: BPS Law
entries.description: The partners at BPS Law have over 40 years’ experience working with credit hire companies, providing advice across the range of regulatory and contract issues that impact the credit hire sector and litigating credit hire claims to maximise the outcome for their clients.

entries.name: Breens
entries.description: Breens Solicitors is based in Southport with a further office in Waterloo, Liverpool. They were first instructed in respect of credit-hire claims as long ago as 1985 when they traded under the name of Wall & Co. The managing partner, Stephen Breen, has been with the practice since then and has a clear understanding of both the legal issues and the challenges facing the credit-hire industry.

entries.name: Brian Berrils
entries.description: Based in Eire, Brian Berrills are a firm of solicitors that assist motorists in the pursuit of damages for personal injury and credit hire following a road collision.

entries.name: Canford Law
entries.description: Canford Law are associate members of the CHO with offices in Welwyn Garden City, Bournemouth and Manchester. They are a relatively newly established, multi discipline Legal Practice, specialising in Insurance Litigation, Family Law and Commercial Litigation. They were formed in 2017 but the Directors claim over 25 years of experience in the Insurance sector.

entries.name: Carpenters Group
entries.description: Carpenters Group are one of the UK’s leading providers of insurance and legal services. They work in partnership with insurers, brokers and CHOs to deliver a variety of fully outsourced claims solutions. Their team has grown to approximately 1,000 staff across seven offices throughout the UK with offices in Liverpool, Leeds, Birkenhead, Haywards Heath and Glasgow.  Predominantly a Claimant practice, Carpenters Group do also have a small defence team and, as many other firms do, operate a series of Chinese wall arrangements to ring fence and protect the IPR on each side of the wall.

entries.name: DGM Solicitors
entries.description: DGM Solicitors provide a range of services including claims arising from road traffic accidents which may involve pursuing claims for damages related to personal injury, post-accident rehabilitation, and credit hire and other associated charges. They work with a number of CHOs but act entirely independently with the aim of achieving the maximum compensation for their lay clients.

entries.name: Garvins Law
entries.description: Garvins Law is part of Edam Group, the largest privately-owned credit hire and post-accident service provider. They offer a range of services associated with personal injury and credit hire recovery and have a very clear purpose, which they say is to look after their clients’ interests confidently, dynamically and in a professional manner.

entries.name: GCS Armagh
entries.description: GCS Solicitors are one of the leading Personal Injury law firms in Northern Ireland and their specialised, ISO:9001 (2015) accredited department can help to recover any losses suffered. Their specialised team, who are members of the Association of Personal Injury Lawyers (APIL) have an excellent record in pursuing road traffic accident claims involving credit hire and their clients have been involved in some of the largest settlements in Northern Ireland.

entries.name: Glaisyers
entries.description: With offices in London and Manchester, Glaisyers LLP has over 40 years of experience supplying business and personal legal advice.  They have been involved in litigation for the recovery of credit-hire charges for over 20 years. They report having a team of skilled claims handlers and solicitors that are specialists in Credit-Hire and highly knowledgeable and adept in the ever-changing case law. Glaisyers have also been a partner of the Auto Bodyshop Professionals (ABP) Club for many years and offer a bespoke Credit Repair Scheme to their clients in the motor industry. They are associate members of the CHO.

entries.name: Jason Prosser
entries.description: Jason Prosser has extensive experience of credit-hire having previously been employed as legal counsel by Helphire (Redde). He now practices as a solicitor in a law firm in Stroud but is an experienced advocate. Before joining Leeper Prosser, Jason ran his own practice representing the victim of road traffic accidents and recovering compensation for damages in the County Court. Jason has taken instructions from several CHOs to recover credit-hire charges in contested cases. His pre-trial preparation, knowledge of the legal arguments posited to resist legitimate claims for credit-hire and his presentational style all combine to deliver effective outcome.

entries.name: JMK Solicitors
entries.description: JMK Solicitors have offices in Newry and Belfast, Northern Ireland. Established in 2003, they have helped tens of thousands of people recover compensation. A team of over sixty people help their clients navigate their route to redress in cases involving personal injury, road traffic accidents, medical accidents, travel claims and claims relating to product liability. They service the whole of Northern Ireland

entries.name: Jones Kinvig
entries.description: Jones Kinvig Lawyers is a firm of solicitors specialising in personal injury litigation, in particular Road Traffic Accidents, Accidents at Work and Trips/Slips.  Their main objective is to provide our clients with a bespoke ‘Red Carpet’ service, committed to ensuring claimants receive maximum compensation in the shortest possible time....

entries.name: Kaizen Law
entries.description: Solicitors at Kaizen Law have been involved in Credit Hire for some time, and consistently seek to find ways and means of combating the many issues that continue to this day to plague CHO’s... SEE MORE

entries.name: Kinetic Law
entries.description: Based in the heart of Lancashire, Kinetic Law operate across the UK from offices in Blackburn and Manchester.  They provide a comprehensive array of services, including acting for those claimants suffering a personal injury claim or requiring a credit hire vehicle.

entries.name: Precision Claim Services
entries.description: Established by Guy Kirkham in 2026, Precision Claim Services is focused on assisting CHOs in the recovery of disputed hire claims and the broader remit of defining and executing a coherent strategy for end-to-end claim recovery.

entries.name: McCarton Turkington Breen Solicitors
entries.description: Located in the heart of Northern Ireland’s legal district, MTB have been supplying a full range of legal services to individuals and businesses since 1977. The firm are associate members of the CHO and Colin Mitchell, a partner, has been a lead solicitor in several notable credit-hire cases.

entries.name: Pabla + Pabla
entries.description: Pabla + Pabla understand that from a credit hire organisation perspective, cash flow and expertise in recovering credit hire charges are important factors. Committed to providing a high-quality expert service both to the claimant and the credit hire organisation the firm has developed a dedicated team of experienced credit hire solicitors, who work on the basis that if a claim is unsuccessful, they will not make any charge.

entries.name: Principia Law
entries.description: Principia Law are a firm of solicitors that specialise in recovering damages in the credit-hire and repair sector. They regularly report success in recovering hire charges in first instance hearings in county courts across the United Kingdom. Details can usually be found in the latest news section of their website or by following Peter Jerman on LinkedIn.

entries.name: Rees Clayton
entries.description: Rees Clayton Solicitors is a professional, proactive, and forward-thinking firm whose values are entrenched throughout the firm... SEE MORE

entries.name: Sheldon Davidson Solicitors
entries.description: Sheldon Davidson Solicitors (SDS) have been established in Whitefield, Manchester since 1997 and are leading specialist personal injury, accident and medical negligence solicitors acting for claimants who are victims of non-fault incidents... SEE MORE

entries.name: Slater Gordon Motor Solutions
entries.description: SGS Business process services UK Ltd trade as Slater Gordon Solutions Motor and supply a range of services covering all key aspects of any motor insurance claim. These include accident management services, claims cost containment, claim recovery, vehicle repair and replacement vehicle management. Slater and Gordon report that they have been established for twenty years and that they manage over 70,000 motor claims annually, providing a wide range of services to include hire, repair, legal representation and rehabilitation.

entries.name: Small Marken
entries.description: Small & Marken Solicitors offer a full range of legal services throughout Northern Ireland. The partners have forged their reputations on their approachability to clients, their dedication and hard work in doing all they can to meet their client’s needs and expectations and delivering a first class service that is second to none and which has helped to cement their reputation for efficiency, professionalism and trust. Amongst other things, the practice specialises in personal injury and credit hire litigation.

entries.name: Stephensons
entries.description: As a full-service law firm Stephensons LLP employ a wide range of legal specialists able to help in handling a commercial or regulatory issue in one of their practice sectors. One of those is the credit-hire industry where the firm understands the perspective of both the CHO and the innocent motor accident victim. Stephensons have a dedicated team who work with many GTA subscribers. They adopt a robust approach towards the recovery of credit-hire charges. They acknowledge the need to settle cases for the correct figure at the earliest point in every case they manage as it progresses in the small claim track/fast track/multi track.

entries.name: True Solicitors
entries.description: True Solicitors have offices in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, Leeds and Birmingham. They were established as Gorman Hamilton Solicitors over 20 years ago and now have 170 dedicated employees that specialise in providing legal advice and support to clients who have suffered due to personal injury, including road traffic accidents, accidents at work, slips, trips and falls, industrial disease and medical negligence.

entries.name: Walker Thomas
entries.description: Walker Thomas are solicitors based in London.  They are specialists in managing credit hire claims, using their expertise and knowledge to provide a high-quality service which can be tailored to suit individual requirements

entries.name: Waterman's
entries.description: Watermans act for one of the larger CHOs who have clients in Scotland. They can service clients across the whole of Scotland with offices in Edinburgh, Glasgow and Dundee.

entries.name: Winn Solicitors
entries.description: Winn Solicitors is one of the country’s leading claims management companies. They have extensive experience in the credit hire market and a pedigree characterised by the numerous awards they have won over the years for their standard of customer care.

entries.name: Allyn Winchester
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call: 2002 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 10 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 545 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Benjamin Williams KC
entries.description: Ben is a commercial practitioner with an emphasis on insurance related disputes. He is also well known for his expertise on the law relating to solicitors, litigation funders, claims management companies and costs, as well as consumer and regulatory law. He is recommended in these areas by the major legal directories and has more experience of credit hire litigation than any one else at the bar.

entries.name: Bobby Shabbir
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call:2001 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 4 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 275 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Christopher Gair
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call: 2000 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 15 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 636 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Christopher Oseykeyre
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call: 2017 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 4 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 266 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Clare Haddon
entries.description: Ropewalk Chambers Year of call : 1999 Geographic area covered : England and Wales Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation : 20 Any noteworthy cases : Best win for a Claimant firm was a £90,000 Credit Hire in Central London County Court, for a written off scooter with a PAV sub £1,000 Of the total credit hire cases handled, less than 5%were for a defendant insurer?

entries.name: Conor Cleland
entries.description: Conor Cleland is highly regarded for his understanding and application of the law relating to credit hire in the market in Northern Ireland

entries.name: Elinor Hoile
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call: 2008 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 11 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 1171 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Guy Vickers
entries.description: Guy is a highly experienced credit hire practitioner.   As a result of his involvement in the Autofocus Appeals litigation, and as junior counsel in the Bent and Stevens appeals, Guy has, over the last few years, become a leading expert in Credit Hire issues and continues to be instructed in Fast and Multi track cases as well as those involving novel and contentious challenges from insurers.  See More...

entries.name: Helen Rutherford
entries.description: Helen was called to the Bar in 2010 and has built a national reputation in credit hire litigation, acting in everything from interlocutory applications to appeals. She is particularly requested for matters concerning the hire of replacement fleet or taxi vehicles and replacement prestige vehicles.  See more...

entries.name: Jack Stuart
entries.description: Ropewalk Chambers Year of Call : 2021 Geographic area covered : England and Wales        Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation : 2 Of the total credit hire cases handled, as at June 2024 60% were for a defendant insurer? 60%

entries.name: Justin Shale
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call:1982 Geographic area covered: country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 12 years Any noteworthy cases: As at June 2024 has conducted more than 536 Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Kathy Anderson
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call: 1997 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 12 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 551 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Ken Delaney
entries.description: Called in 1996, Ken has been a fixture in claimant credit hire litigation for more than 20 years. He is on the top of the list of go-to counsel for most CHOs and solicitors and has a reputation for his knowledge and understanding of the issues but, perhaps more importantly, for his ability to take judges that have a lack of credit hire knowledge back to first principles. He appears across the country in Fast and Multi track matters.

entries.name: Mark Maitland
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call:1988 Geographic area covered: country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 11 years As ay June 2024 has conducted more than 769  credit hire cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Matt Barnett
entries.description: Ropewalk Chambers Year of Call : 2017 Geographic area covered : England and Wales Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation : 7 Claims number of successful claims in excess of £100,000 Of the total credit hire cases handled, 50% were for a defendant insurer? 50%

entries.name: Michael Cameron Mowat
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call: 2017 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 5 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 481 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Neil Hinton
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call: 1997 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 11 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 380 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Neil Johnstone
entries.description: Kings Bench ChambersYear of Call: 2015 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 6 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 447 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Neil Whittle
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call:2018 Geographic area covered: country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 9 years As at June 2024, has conducted more than 495 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Nick White
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call: 2000 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 6 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 311 Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Pete Bush
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call: 2008 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 15 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 883 Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Peter Savory
entries.description: Farrar's Building Year of Call: 2018 Geographic area covered: Country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 6 years Of the total credit hire cases handled, 40% were defendant work

entries.name: Philip Davy
entries.description: Ropewalk Chambers Year of Call : 2009 Geographic area covered : Midlands Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation : 13 Of the total credit hire cases handled, 40% were for a defendant insurer

entries.name: Richard Wilcock
entries.description: Exchange Chambers Year of Call: 2012 Geographic area covered: Nationwide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 25 years plus (as Counsel and solicitor) Any noteworthy cases: Burdis v Livesey (Clark v Ardington etc) whilst engaged as solicitor via Accident Assistance. 1% of total credit hire cases handled were for a defendant insurer.

entries.name: Rod Hine
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers, Bournemouth Year of Call:1985 Geographic area covered: country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 11 years Any noteworthy cases: As at June 2024 reports having conducted more than 177 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled Nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Samuel Ford
entries.description: Ropewalk Chambera Year of Call : 2021 Geographic area covered : England and Wales (Midlands only for Small Claims Track) Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation : 2.5 Of the total credit hire cases handled, 30% were for a defendant insurer but, as at June 2024, reports currently based towards claimant

entries.name: Stuart MacDonald
entries.description: Kings Bench Chambers Year of Call:2009 Geographic area covered: country wide Number of Years experience in credit hire litigation: 9 years As at June 2024 has conducted more than 420 cases Of the total credit hire cases handled, nil% were defendant work

entries.name: Stuart Nicol
entries.description: Stuart Nicol is a highly experienced counsel with 25 year’s experience. He is a leading junior in the field of Credit Hire representing both Claimants and Defendants. He successfully represented the Claimant in the case of Cheung v. UK Insurance [2015]. He was junior counsel with Richard Spearman QC in Clayton v. EUI Ltd. in the recent Court of Appeal decision which was conjoined with McBride v. UK Insurance

entries.name: Atlantic Chambers
entries.description: Credit-hire owes much of its history to the growth of CHOs in the North West of England. It is no surprise, therefore, that centres of excellence and expertise have evolved in Liverpool, Manchester and Leeds. Highly recommended are Kenneth Delaney, Shannon Eastwood and Olivia Cox.

entries.name: Exchange Chambers
entries.description: Exchange Chambers work from locations in Liverpool, Manchester and Leeds. Of note are Guy Vickers, a barrister with 33 years call who has acted, and continues to act, on instructions from a number of leading CHOs at first instance, in high value claims or those involving a particularly complex technical defence, or on appeal to the High Court and Court of Appeal. Helen Rutherford is also at Exchange Chambers. She is highly recommended by a large CHO and several solicitors who praised her consistent and impressive performance in credit-hire litigation. She is regularly instructed in both small claim and fast track hearings and has a masterful grasp of the arguments and issues raised.

entries.name: Kings Bench Chambers
entries.description: King’s Bench Chambers are based in Bournemouth. That sounds an odd location for a CHO looking to find counsel with credit-hire expertise, but they are instructed by several national solicitors involved in credit-hire litigation. They handle instructions in small claims, fast track and multi-track litigation and will appear in all courts throughout the Circuits of England and Wales. Recommended are Peet Bush, Elinor Hoile, Thomas Waldron, Christopher Gair and Kathleen Anderson

entries.name: Kings Chambers
entries.description: Andrew Hogan practises from Kings Chambers in Manchester, Leeds and Birmingham and has been involved in advising upon and arguing credit hire claims for 20 years. He travels nationally across England and Wales: from Truro to Newcastle or wherever the work takes him. He publishes an engaging blog at www.credithirebarrister.com.

entries.name: No 5 Chambers
entries.description: No 5 Chambers  provide a nationwide service from offices in Birmingham, London, Bristol and Leicester. From experience, members have extensive experience of supplying advice and representation in all aspects of credit-hire claims on both a Claimant and Defendant basis ranging from small claims through to multi-track cases. They are experienced in arguing the technical issues that may arise in credit-hire claims including managing arguments about mitigation, intervention and subrogation as well as the specific challenges relating to recovery and storage, length of hire, issues relating to the recoverable rate as well as disputes founded on allegation of fraud, contractual errors or collateral agreements. Glen Willetts is highly recommended.

entries.name: DPR Solutions
entries.description: DPR Solutions Ltd is a provider of bespoke rebuttal statements used to counter basic hire rates evidence adduced by third party insurers and defendant solicitors in credit-hire cases.

entries.name: HP Consultants
entries.description: HP Consultants provide a range of solutions to the credit hire industry. These include a members advice line, recovery of credit hire claims, a claim handling service, advice on cross hire and vehicle leasing and also the preparation of BHR reports.

entries.name: AMT
entries.description: AMT offer a range of lease and rental solutions utilising their fleet of premium, specialist and luxury vehicles makes them a useful partner for any credit-hire company looking either for a short-term solution to resolve an unexpected capacity issue or a more medium-term answer to funding part of their fleet on flexible terms.

entries.name: Europcar
entries.description: Europcar’s relationship with credit-hire can be charted back to the first iteration of the GTA. Since then, they have played no direct part in offering credit-hire services direct to the public. They have, however, engaged in a variety of different supply relationships with several credit-hire operators.

entries.name: Thrifty
entries.description: Thrifty have experience of working with credit-hire operators and understand the challenges faced by CHOs.

entries.name: Accident Credit Group
entries.description: Specialist funders of credit repair debt to the credit hire and repair industry.

entries.name: Leumi
entries.description: Leumi offer trade financing and invoice discounting facilities and are one of a few banks that have experience in the credit-hire marketplace. They specialise in structured asset backed lending facilities from £1m to £35m

entries.name: 96 SAE
entries.description: A consultancy owned by Steve Evans which offers a variety of services for those engaged with the credit hire industry. Those services include advice relating to strategy, funding, claim recovery and external diligence.

entries.name: Compliancy Services
entries.description: Compliancy Services is a specialist compliance consultancy that was founded in 2003 in response to the increase in regulation across the financial services sector. They have considerable experience working with Claim Management Companies and CHOs to minimise the regulatory burden and to ensure that compliance is an enabler to business growth. Iain Stephen is joint CEO and has been involved with the credit-hire industry since 2005.  He sits on the Executive Committee of the CHO trade body and has supplied training, consultancy and ongoing monitoring for several CHOs over the years.

entries.name: Fusion
entries.description: Fusion can provide direct support services to CHOs as well as assessing compliance with the GTA, MoJ and FCA. They aim to drive operational efficiencies which requires an understanding of exactly what it is that CHOs need to achieve, as well as what insurers expect.

entries.name: Clifford James
entries.description: Clifford James offer a service that allows clients to recover damages for diminution or the loss in value of their motor vehicle following a road traffic accident.

entries.name: Incident Management Solutions
entries.description: IMS started as an engineering company but now offer individually tailored solutions to the vehicle motor claims industry. Founded in 2006 they now deal with over 60,000 claims a year and specialise in helping clients deal with the vehicle, whether it be in respect of repair, total loss, cash in lieu or just engineering. They claim to offer unrivalled professionalism, efficiency and market leading solutions all built around an ethical approach.

entries.name: Verius
entries.description: Managed by James Bilham, Verius provides leading-edge risk and counter fraud services for the credit hire industry.

entries.name: GTA Technical Committee
entries.description: The entity responsible for managing the ABI GTA protocol comprising a mix of insurers and CHO representatives

entries.name: Law Brief Publishing
entries.description: Law Brief Publishing has grown to be the UK's leading online personal injury journal and supplies new articles each month on a wide variety of topics from liability and quantum to costs, civil procedure, credit-hire and fraud. It has an archive of well over one thousand articles

entries.name: Linkfield Corporate Services
entries.description: Linkfield Corporate Solutions is an insurance broker with experience supplying underwriting solutions in both the self-drive and credit-hire market from a range of insurers.

entries.name: The CHO
entries.description: The trade body representing the credit hire industry on the ABI GTA Technical Committee
